phonetic: 藢n蓲n蓹d蕭蓹藞ne蓹ri蓹n; 藢n蓹蕣n蓹d蕭蓹藞ne蓹ri蓹n
phoneticUs: 藢no蕣n蓹d蕭蓹藞neri蓹n; 藢n蓱藧n蓹d蕭蓹藞neri蓹n
meaning: adj.涔濆崄澶氬瞾鐨刵.涔濆崄澶氬瞾
examples: If the Fair Folk are not actually deathless, they are certainly much longer-lived than any mortal man, even our nonagenarian king.
We can all appreciate a wizened visage on our favorite nonagenarian that is. Wrinkles look anything but wise when they show up on a relatively young person who smokes.
But if you had to look to a nonagenarian for direction, you could do much worse than Harman, a technology pioneer and lover of the arts whose life has taken as many twists as a telenovela.
